definitions.text: At what time? At which time? Upon which occasion or circumstance? Used to introduce direct or indirect questions about time.
examples: Do you know when they arrived?

definitions.text: Used after a noun or noun phrase in isolation to express impatience with an anticipated future event.
examples: The site's all bugged. Fix when?

definitions.text: At an earlier time and under different, usually less favorable, circumstances.
examples: He's mister high and mighty now, but I remember him when.

definitions.text: At (or as soon as) that time that; at the (or any and every) time that; if.
examples: Pavlov's dogs salivate when [i.e. at any and every time that] they hear a bell.
When [i.e. at any and every time that] he speaks to her, he is always polite.

definitions.text: During the time that; at the time of the action of the following clause or participle phrase.
examples: They dream when [i.e. during the time that] they sleep.
I'm happiest when [during the time that, or at any time that] I’m working.

definitions.text: At what time; at which time.
examples: I am here till Friday, when [i.e. at which time] I leave for Senegal.
I was just walking down the street, when [i.e. at which time] all of a sudden it started to rain.
Since when do I need your permission?

definitions.text: The time at which something happens.
examples: A good article will cover the who, the what, the when, the where, the why and the how.

definitions.text: That's enough: a command asking someone to stop adding something, especially an ingredient or portion of food or drink; used in, or as if in, literal response to 'Say when'.
